samples.xml_description:
Implement a series-parallel conversion circuit. It receives a serial input signal "din_serial" along with a control signal "din_valid" indicating the validity of the input data. The module operates on the rising edge of the clock signal "clk" and uses a synchronous design. The input din_serial is a single-bit data, and when the module receives 8 input data, the output dout_parallel outputs the 8-bit data(The serial input values are sequentially placed in dout_parallel from the most significant bit to the least significant bit), and the dout_valid is set to 1. Clock signal. Reset signal (active low). Serial input data. Validity signal for input data. Parallel output data. Validity signal for the output data. ADD SUB SRA SRL valid dout din din_valid counter

Receive 8-bit input data through the serial input port din_serial and store it in the register din. The register din is updated in a sequential manner, with each new bit being shifted into the most significant position.

When the counter cnt reaches 8, the parallel output dout_parallel is updated to reflect the contents of the register din. The output dout_valid is set to 1 to indicate that the output data is valid.

Reset the module by asserting the reset signal rst_n. When the reset signal is low, the counter cnt is set to 0, and the output dout_valid is set to 0.

samples.xml_description:
Design a 16-bit divider. Clock. Reset. 1-bit indicates if the operation is signed (1) or unsigned (0). 16-bit input signal representing the dividend for division. 16-bit input signal representing the divisor for division. 1-bit indicates that a valid operation request is present. 1-bit indicates that the 16-bit output result has already been acknowledged. 1-bit output signal indicating the result is valid and ready. 16-bit the output containing the remainder in the upper 8 bits and the quotient in the lower 8 bits. 0 1 2 3 4 1-bit register to track the completion of the division operation. 16-bit register to store the negative divisor. 32-bit register to store the shift register. 16-bit register to store the subtraction result. 17-bit register to store the carry. 5-bit register to store the counter. 1-bit register to start the counter. 1-bit register to store the result sign. 16-bit register to store the absolute value of the dividend. 16-bit register to store the absolute value of the divisor.

Operation Start: When opn_valid is high and res_valid is low, the module saves the inputs dividend and divisor. It initializes the shift register sr with the absolute value of the dividend shifted left by one bit. It also sets neg_divisor to the negated absolute value of the divisor and sets the counter cnt to 1 and start_cnt to 1 to begin the division process.

Division Process: If start_cnt is high, the module performs the following steps: If the counter cnt reaches 16 (most significant bit of cnt is set), the division is complete. In this case, cnt and start_cnt are cleared, and the module updates the shift register sr with the final remainder and quotient. Otherwise, the counter cnt is incremented, and the shift register sr is updated based on the subtraction result: The module computes the subtraction of neg_divisor from the upper 16 bits of sr. It uses a multiplexer to select the appropriate result based on the carry-out: If there is a carry (borrow), the module sets the next bit in sr to 1; otherwise, it sets it to 0. The module updates sr by shifting left and inserting the carry.

Result Validity: The res_valid signal is managed based on the reset signal, the counter, and whether the result has been consumed. res_valid should be updated directly, without passing through intermediate registers. When rst is high, res_valid is set to zero. If cnt reaches 16, res_valid is set to 1. If res_valid and res_ready are both high, res_valid is set to 0. Otherwise, res_valid is set to its previous value.

samples.xml_description:
Converts 8-bit data to 16-bit data. Clock signal. Active-low reset signal. Input signal indicating valid data. 8-bit input data. Output signal indicating valid data. 16-bit output data. Parameter Description:
N/A type variables:
data_lock: 8-bit register to store the first input data.
flag: 1-bit flag to indicate the status of data processing.
Sequential logic block triggered on the positive edge of the clock or the negative edge of the reset signal.
When the reset signal is active (negedge rst_n), the flag is set to 0.
If valid_in is active and flag is 0, the input data is stored in data_lock, and flag is set to 1.
If valid_in is active and flag is 1, the output data is constructed from data_lock and data_in, and flag is set to 0.

Algorithm:

On the positive edge of the clock or the negative edge of the reset signal:

if (!rst_n)

    flag = 0;

else if (valid_in && !flag)

    data_lock = data_in;

    flag = 1;

else if (valid_in && flag)

    data_out = {data_lock, data_in};

    valid_out = 1;

    flag = 0;

else

    valid_out = 0;

samples.xml_description:
<module id="pe">
    <description>Implement a Multiplying Accumulator for 32bit integer. In the MAC_PE, there is a register that stores the partial sum (the intermediate accumulation result), and in each cycle, the result of “a multiplied by b” will be accumulated in this register, and the “c” shows the value of the register.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock.</input>
        <input id="rst">Reset signal. Defined as 1 for reset and 0 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="a" width_description="[31:0]">32-bit input operand A.</input>
        <input id="b" width_description="[31:0]">32-bit input operand B.</input>
        <output id="c" width_description="[31:0]">32-bit output representing the accumulated result.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="MAC_PE">Multiply and accumulate in a PE.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>Accumulator Register: The module includes a register, c, to store the accumulated result. On the positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the positive edge of the reset signal (rst), the module updates the register. If the reset signal (rst) is high, indicating a reset condition, the register (c) is set to 0. If the reset signal (rst) is low, the register (c) is updated by adding the product of the input operands (a and b) to its current value. Multiplication and Accumulation: Inside the always block, the module performs the multiplication and accumulation operation. If the reset signal (rst) is high, the register (c) is set to 0. If the reset signal (rst) is low, the module adds the product of the input operands (a and b) to the register (c).</implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
<module id="traffic_light">
    <description>Implement a traffic light with red, yellow, green indicators and a pedestrian button. In normal conditions, the motor vehicle lane light should be green for 60 clock cycles, yellow for 5 clock cycles, and red for 10 clock cycles. When the pedestrian button is pressed, if the remaining green time is greater than 10 clocks, it is shortened to 10 clocks. If it is less than 10 clocks, it remains unchanged. The lane light and the sidewalk light should be paired: when the lane light is green or yellow, the sidewalk light is red; when the lane light is red, the sidewalk light is green.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="pass_request">Request signal for allowing vehicles to pass.</input>
        <output id="red">Red output signal.</output>
        <output id="yellow">Yellow output signal.</output>
        <output id="green">Green output signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="idle">Idle state.</parameter>
        <parameter id="s1_red">State 1, red signal.</parameter>
        <parameter id="s2_yellow">State 2, yellow signal.</parameter>
        <parameter id="s3_green">State 3, green signal.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <sequential_logic id="state">
            <description>
                The state machine transitions between different states based on the current state and the pass_request signal. 
                In the idle state, the state machine transitions to the s1_red state. 
                In the s1_red state, the state machine transitions to the s3_green state after 3 clock cycles. 
                In the s2_yellow state, the state machine transitions to the s1_red state after 3 clock cycles. 
                In the s3_green state, the state machine transitions to the s2_yellow state after 3 clock cycles.
            </description>
        </sequential_logic>
        <sequential_logic id="cnt">
            <description>
                The counter is reset to 10 when rst_n is low. 
                If the pass_request signal is active and the green signal is active, the counter is set to 10. 
                If the green signal is inactive and the previous green signal (p_green) was active, the counter is set to 60. 
                If the yellow signal is inactive and the previous yellow signal (p_yellow) was active, the counter is set to 5. 
                If the red signal is inactive and the previous red signal (p_red) was active, the counter is set to 10. 
                Otherwise, the counter is decremented by 1.
            </description>
        </sequential_logic>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <description>
            The traffic light controller has three states: idle, s1_red, and s2_yellow. 
            In the idle state, the state machine transitions to the s1_red state and sets the red signal to 1. 
            In the s1_red state, the state machine transitions to the s3_green state after 3 clock cycles and sets the green signal to 1. 
            In the s2_yellow state, the state machine transitions to the s1_red state after 3 clock cycles and sets the red signal to 1. 
            The counter is reset to 10 when rst_n is low. 
            If the pass_request signal is active and the green signal is active, the counter is set to 10. 
            If the green signal is inactive and the previous green signal (p_green) was active, the counter is set to 60. 
            If the yellow signal is inactive and the previous yellow signal (p_yellow) was active, the counter is set to 5. 
            If the red signal is inactive and the previous red signal (p_red) was active, the counter is set to 10. 
            Otherwise, the counter is decremented by 1.
        </description>
    </implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
Implement a 16-bit unsigned multiplier using shift and accumulate operations. Clock signal. Active-low reset signal. Chip enable signal for multiplication. Multiplicand input. Multiplier input. Product output. Completion flag for multiplication. Shift count register. Shift count register. Multiplication completion flag. Product register. Multiplicand register. Multiplier register.

On every positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the shift count register (i) is updated.

When the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, the shift count register (i) is set to 0.

When the start signal is active (start) and the shift count register (i) is less than 17, the shift count register (i) increments by 1.

When the start signal is inactive (!start), the shift count register (i) is reset to 0.

On every positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the multiplication completion flag (done_r) is updated.

When the reset signal (rst_n) is low, the multiplication completion flag (done_r) is set to 0.

When the shift count register (i) is equal to 16, indicating the completion of the multiplication operation, the multiplication completion flag (done_r) is set to 1.

When the shift count register (i) is equal to 17, the multiplication completion flag (done_r) is reset to 0.

On every positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the module performs the shift and accumulate operation.

When the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, the multiplicand register (areg), multiplier register (breg), and product register (yout_r) are reset to 0.

When the start signal is active (start), the module starts the multiplication operation.

When the shift count register (i) is 0, the multiplicand (ain) and multiplier (bin) are stored in the respective registers (areg and breg).

For shift counts greater than 0 and less than 17, if the bit at position i-1 in the multiplicand register (areg) is high, the product register (yout_r) accumulates the shifted value of the multiplier register (breg) by shifting it left by i-1 positions and appending zeros at the least significant bit positions.

The product output (yout) is assigned the value of the product register (yout_r).

samples.xml_description: Implement an unsigned 8bit multiplier based on pipelining processing. Clock. Active-low reset. Input enable bit for multiplication. Input multiplicand. Input multiplier. Output enable bit for multiplication. Output product. 8 Reset the internal registers. if (!rst_n) begin mul_en_out_reg = 0; mul_a_reg = 0; mul_b_reg = 0; sum = 0; mul_out_reg = 0; end Update the mul_en_out_reg with the mul_en_in signal. mul_en_out_reg[&N;] = mul_en_in; Update the mul_a_reg and mul_b_reg with the mul_a and mul_b. mul_a_reg[&N;] = mul_a; mul_b_reg[&N;] = mul_b; Generate the partial products based on the bits of the multiplier. assign temp[0] = mul_b_reg[0] ? mul_a_reg : 0; assign temp[1] = mul_b_reg[1] ? mul_a_reg : 0; assign temp[2] = mul_b_reg[2] ? mul_a_reg : 0; assign temp[3] = mul_b_reg[3] ? mul_a_reg : 0; assign temp[4] = mul_b_reg[4] ? mul_a_reg : 0; assign temp[5] = mul_b_reg[5] ? mul_a_reg : 0; assign temp[6] = mul_b_reg[6] ? mul_a_reg : 0; assign temp[7] = mul_b_reg[7] ? mul_a_reg : 0; Calculate the partial sum based on the generated partial products. always @(posedge clk) begin if (mul_en_out_reg) begin sum[0] = temp[0][7:0]; sum[1] = sum[0] + temp[1][7:0]; sum[2] = sum[1] + temp[2][7:0]; sum[3] = sum[2] + temp[3][7:0]; sum[4] = sum[3] + temp[4][7:0]; sum[5] = sum[4] + temp[5][7:0]; sum[6] = sum[5] + temp[6][7:0]; sum[7] = sum[6] + temp[7][7:0]; end end Calculate the final product based on the partial sum. always @(posedge clk) begin if (mul_en_out_reg) begin mul_out_reg = mul_out_reg + sum[7]; end end Assign the output based on the mul_en_out_reg signal. assign mul_out = mul_en_out_reg ? mul_out_reg : 0;

samples.xml_description:
Convert parallel data to serial data. Clock. Reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. 4-bit parallel data input. Valid signal indicating the availability of serial output. Serial output representing the converted data. 4

Parallel data is converted to serial data. The most significant bit of the parallel input is assigned to the serial output (dout). On each clock cycle, if the counter (cnt) is 3, indicating the last bit of the parallel input, the module updates the data register (data) with the parallel input (d), resets the counter (cnt) to 0, and sets the valid signal (valid) to 1.

Otherwise, the module increments the counter (cnt) by 1, sets the valid signal (valid) to 0, and shifts the data register (data) one bit to the left, with the most significant bit shifted to the least significant bit. Counter Register: If the reset signal (rst_n) is high, the register (cnt) is incremented by 1.

<module id="div_16bit">
    <description>Implement a 16-bit divider module, the dividend is 16-bit and the divider is 8-bit in combinational logic. Extract the higher bits of the dividend, matching the bit width of the divisor. Compare these bits with the divisor: if the dividend bits are greater, set the quotient to 1, otherwise set it to 0, and use the difference as the remainder. Concatenate the remainder with the highest remaining 1-bit of the dividend, and repeat the process until all dividend bits are processed.</description>

<module id="fsm">
    <description>Implement a Mealy FSM detection circuit that detects a single-bit input IN. When the input is 10011, output MATCH is 1, and MATCH is 0 in other cases. Support for continuous input and loop detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="IN">Input signal to the FSM.</input>
        <input id="CLK">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="RST">Reset signal to initialize the FSM.</input>
        <output id="MATCH">Output signal indicating a match condition based on the FSM state.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="S0">Initial state of the FSM.</parameter>
        <parameter id="S1">State of the FSM when the input is 1.</parameter>
        <parameter id="S2">State of the FSM when the input is 0.</parameter>
        <parameter id="S3">State of the FSM when the input is 1.</parameter>
        <parameter id="S4">State of the FSM when the input is 1.</parameter>
        <parameter id="S5">State of the FSM when the input is 0.</parameter>
        <parameter id="S6">State of the FSM when the input is 0.</parameter>
        <parameter id="S7">State of the FSM when the input is 1.</parameter>
        <parameter id="S8">State of the FSM when the input is 1.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="state" type="sequential_logic">Current state of the FSM.</reg>
        <reg id="next_state" type="sequential_logic">Next state of the FSM.</reg>
        <reg id="match_reg" type="sequential_logic">Register to store the MATCH output.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        <sequential_logic>
            <always>
                <trigger>
                    <input id="IN" description="Input signal to the FSM." />
                    <input id="CLK" description="Clock signal used for synchronous operation." />
                    <input id="RST" description="Reset signal to initialize the FSM." />
                </trigger>
                <action>
                    <if>
                        <condition>
                            <input id="RST" />
                        </condition>
                        <then>
                            <set>
                                <output id="MATCH" value="0" />
                            </set>
                        </then>
                    </if>
                    <else>
                        <set>
                            <output id="MATCH" value="match_reg" />
                        </set>
                    </else>
                </action>
            </always>
            <always>
                <trigger>
                    <input id="CLK" description="Clock signal used for synchronous operation." />
                    <input id="RST" description="Reset signal to initialize the FSM." />
                </trigger>
                <action>
                    <if>
                        <condition>
                            <input id="RST" />
                        </condition>
                        <then>
                            <set>
                                <output id="state" value="S0" />
                            </set>
                        </then>
                    </if>
                    <else>
                        <set>
                            <output id="state" value="next_state" />
                        </set>
                    </else>
                </action>
            </always>
        </sequential_logic>
        <combinational_logic>
            <always>
                <trigger>
                    <input id="state" description="Current state of the FSM." />
                    <input id="IN" description="Input signal to the FSM." />
                </trigger>
                <action>
                    <case>
                        <condition>
                            <input id="state" />
                        </condition>
                        <when>
                            <condition>
                                <parameter id="S0" />
                            </condition>
                            <then>
                                <set>
                                    <output id="next_state" value="S1" />
                                    <output id="match_reg" value="0" />
                                </set>
                            </then>
                        </when>
                        <when>
                            <condition>
                                <parameter id="S1" />
                            </condition>
                            <then>
                                <set>
                                    <output id="next_state" value="S2" />
                                    <output id="match_reg" value="0" />
                                </set>
                            </then>
                        </when>
                        <when>
                            <condition>
                                <parameter id="S2" />
                            </condition>
                            <then>
                                <set>
                                    <output id="next_state" value="S3" />
                                    <output id="match_reg" value="0" />
                                </set>
                            </then>
                        </when>
                        <when>
                            <condition>
                                <parameter id="S3" />

                            <then>
                                <set>

                                </set>
                            </then>
                        </when>
                        <when>
                            <condition>
                                <parameter id="S4" />
                            </condition>
                            <then>
                                <set>
                                    <output id="next_state" value="S0" />
                                    <output id="match_reg" value="IN" />
                                </set>
                            </then>
                        </when>
                        <when>
                            <condition>
                                <parameter id="S5" />
                            </condition>
                            <then>
                                <set>
                                    <output id="next_state" value="S0" />
                                    <output id="match_reg" value="0" />
                                </set>
                            </then>
                        </when>
                        <when>
                            <condition>
                                <parameter id="S6" />
                            </condition>
                            <then>
                                <set>
                                    <output id="next_state" value="S0" />
                                    <output id="match_reg" value="0" />
                                </set>
                            </then>
                        </when>
                        <when>
                            <condition>
                                <parameter id="S7" />
                            </condition>
                            <then>
                                <set>
                                    <output id="next_state" value="S0" />
                                    <output id="match_reg" value="IN" />
                                </set>
                            </then>
                        </when>
                        <when>
                            <condition>

                                    <output id="match_reg" value="0" />
                                </set>

                </action>
            </always>
        </combinational_logic>

<module id="alu">
    <description>Implement an ALU for a 32-bit MIPS-ISA CPU.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="a" width_description="[31:0]">A 32-bit input operand.</input>
        <input id="b" width_description="[31:0]">A 32-bit input operand.</input>
        <input id="aluc" width_description="[5:0]">ALU control signal.</input>
        <output id="r" width_description="[31:0]">A 32-bit output representing the result of the operation.</output>
        <output id="zero">A 1-bit output indicating whether the result is zero.</output>
        <output id="carry">A 1-bit output indicating whether a carry occurred during the operation.</output>
        <output id="negative">A 1-bit output indicating whether the result is negative.</output>
        <output id="overflow">A 1-bit output indicating whether an overflow occurred during the operation.</output>
        <output id="flag">A 1-bit output representing a general flag, which is set based on specific operations (SLT and SLTU).</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="ADD">6'b100000</parameter>
        <parameter id="ADDU">6'b100001</parameter>
        <parameter id="SUB">6'b100010</parameter>
        <parameter id="SUBU">6'b100011</parameter>
        <parameter id="AND">6'b100100</parameter>
        <parameter id="OR">6'b100101</parameter>
        <parameter id="XOR">6'b100110</parameter>
        <parameter id="NOR">6'b100111</parameter>
        <parameter id="SLT">6'b101010</parameter>
        <parameter id="SLTU">6'b101011</parameter>
        <parameter id="SLL">6'b000000</parameter>
        <parameter id="SRL">6'b000010</parameter>
        <parameter id="SRA">6'b000011</parameter>
        <parameter id="SLLV">6'b000100</parameter>
        <parameter id="SRLV">6'b000110</parameter>
        <parameter id="SRAV">6'b000111</parameter>
        <parameter id="LUI">6'b001111</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>Uses parameters to define the control signals for various operations, such as ADD, SUB, AND, OR, etc. 
The module assigns the input operands to the signed wires and the output result (r) to the lower 32 bits of the register (res[31:0]). The flag output is determined based on the control signal (aluc) and is set to '1' when the operation is SLT or SLTU, and 'z' (high-impedance) otherwise. The zero output is set to '1' when the result is all zeros, and '0' otherwise.
Inside the always block, a case statement is used to perform the appropriate operation based on the control signal (aluc). The result is assigned to the register (res) accordingly. For shift operations (SLL, SRL, SRA, SLLV, SRLV, SRAV), the shift amount is determined by the value of 'a' or 'a[4:0]'. For the LUI operation, the upper 16 bits of 'a' are concatenated with 16 zeros to form the result.
If the control signal (aluc) does not match any defined operation, the result is assigned as 'z' (high-impedance).</implementation>
</module>

<module id="edge_detect">
    <description>Implement a module for edge detection.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="a">Input signal.</input>
        <output id="rise">Output signal indicating a rising edge.</output>
        <output id="down">Output signal indicating a falling edge.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter name="state0">Initial state.</parameter>
        <parameter name="state1">State when rising edge detected.</parameter>
        <parameter name="state2">State when falling edge detected.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="current_state">Current state.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="next_state">Next state.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="rise_reg">Rising edge output register.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="down_reg">Falling edge output register.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        The edge_detect module detects rising and falling edges in the input signal a and generates corresponding output signals rise and down. 
        <sequential>
            On the positive edge of the clock signal clk or the negative edge of the reset signal rst_n, the current state is updated. 
            If the reset signal is active (low), the current state is set to state0 (initial state). 
            Otherwise, the current state is updated to the next state.
        </sequential>
        <combinational>
            The next state is determined based on the current state and the input signal a. 
            If the current state is state0, the next state is state1 if a is high (1), or state2 if a is low (0). 
            If the current state is state1, the next state is state0 if a is low (0), or state1 if a is high (1). 
            If the current state is state2, the next state is state0 if a is high (1), or state2 if a is low (0).
        </combinational>
        <combinational>
            The rise and down output signals are set based on the current state. 
            In state1, rise_reg is set to 1, indicating a rising edge detection. 
            In state2, down_reg is set to 1, indicating a falling edge detection. 
            In all other states, both output signals are set to 0.
        </combinational>
        <sequential>
            The output signals rise and down are updated on the positive edge of the clock signal clk. 
            rise is set to the value of rise_reg, and down is set to the value of down_reg.
        </sequential>
        <sequential>
            The rise_reg and down_reg output registers are reset to 0 on the positive edge of the clock signal clk or the negative edge of the reset signal rst_n. 
            If the reset signal is active (low), both registers are set to 0. 
            Otherwise, the registers are updated based on the current state.
        </sequential>
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="multi_pipe_4bit">
    <description>Implement a 4-bit unsigned number pipeline multiplier.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[3:0]">Multiplicand.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[3:0]">Multiplier.</input>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[7:0]">Product.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter name="size">4</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
    <task>
        <description>Extend input signals.</description>
        <parameter_description>
            <parameter name="i">Loop index.</parameter>
        </parameter_description>
        <implementation>
            <assign name="extend_a">Concatenate (mul_a, size zeros)</assign>
            <assign name="extend_b">Concatenate (mul_b, size zeros)</assign>
        </implementation>
    </task>
    <assign name="partial_product[0]">
        <description>Calculate partial product for bit position 0.</description>
        <if>
            <condition>bit_mul_b[0] == 1</condition>
            <then>
                <assign name="extend_a[3:0]">Left shift mul_a by 0 positions</assign>
            </then>
            <else>
                <assign name="0">0</assign>
            </then>
        </if>
    </assign>
    <assign name="partial_product[1]">
        <description>Calculate partial product for bit position 1.</description>
        <if>
            <condition>bit_mul_b[1] == 1</condition>
            <then>
                <assign name="extend_a[3:0]">Left shift mul_a by 1 position</assign>
            </then>
            <else>
                <assign name="0">0</assign>
            </then>
        </if>
    </assign>
    <assign name="partial_product[2]">
        <description>Calculate partial product for bit position 2.</description>
        <if>
            <condition>bit_mul_b[2] == 1</condition>
            <then>
                <assign name="extend_a[3:0]">Left shift mul_a by 2 positions</assign>
            </then>
            <else>
                <assign name="0">0</assign>
            </then>
        </if>
    </assign>
    <assign name="partial_product[3]">
        <description>Calculate partial product for bit position 3.</description>
        <if>
            <condition>bit_mul_b[3] == 1</condition>
            <then>
                <assign name="extend_a[3:0]">Left shift mul_a by 3 positions</assign>
            </then>
            <else>
                <assign name="0">0</assign>
            </then>
        </if>
    </assign>
    <assign name="sum[0]">
        <description>Calculate sum for stage 0.</description>
        <if>
            <condition>rst_n == 0</condition>
            <then>
                <assign name="0">0</assign>
            </then>
            <else>
                <assign name="partial_product[0]">partial_product[0]</assign>
            </then>
        </if>
    </assign>
    <assign name="sum[1]">
        <description>Calculate sum for stage 1.</description>
        <if>
            <condition>rst_n == 0</condition>
            <then>
                <assign name="0">0</assign>
            </then>
            <else>
                <assign name="partial_product[1] + partial_product[0]">partial_product[1] + partial_product[0]</assign>
            </then>
        </if>
    </assign>
    <assign name="sum[2]">
        <description>Calculate sum for stage 2.</description>
        <if>
            <condition>rst_n == 0</condition>
            <then>
                <assign name="0">0</assign>
            </then>
            <else>
                <assign name="partial_product[2] + partial_product[1] + partial_product[0]">partial_product[2] + partial_product[1] + partial_product[0]</assign>
            </then>
        </if>
    </assign>
    <assign name="sum[3]">
        <description>Calculate sum for stage 3.</description>
        <if>
            <condition>rst_n == 0</condition>
            <then>
                <assign name="0">0</assign>
            </then>
            <else>
                <assign name="partial_product[3] + partial_product[2] + partial_product[1] + partial_product[0]">partial_product[3] + partial_product[2] + partial_product[1] + partial_product[0]</assign>
            </then>
        </if>
    </assign>
    <assign name="mul_out">
        <description>Calculate final product.</description>
        <if>
            <condition>rst_n == 0</condition>
            <then>
                <assign name="0">0</assign>
            </then>
            <else>
                <assign name="sum[3] + partial_product[2] + partial_product[1] + partial_product[0]">sum[3] + partial_product[2] + partial_product[1] + partial_product[0]</assign>
            </then>
        </if>
    </assign>
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="calendar">
    <description>Module name: calendar
    Input ports: CLK: Clock input
    RST: Active high reset signal
    Output ports: Hours: 6-bit output representing the current hours
    Mins: 6-bit output representing the current minutes
    Secs: 6-bit output representing the current seconds
    Implementation: The calendar module uses three always blocks to update the values of seconds, minutes, and hours based on the clock signal and reset signal.
    The first always block triggers on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge CLK) or the positive edge of the reset signal (posedge RST). It checks if the reset signal is active (RST) and sets the seconds value (Secs) to 0. If the seconds value is 59, it wraps around and sets the seconds value to 0. Otherwise, it increments the seconds value by 1.
    The second always block triggers on the positive edge of the clock signal or the positive edge of the reset signal. It handles the minutes value (Mins). If the reset signal is active, it sets the minutes value to 0. If both the minutes and seconds values are 59, it wraps around and sets the minutes value to 0. If the seconds value is 59, it increments the minutes value by 1. Otherwise, it keeps the minutes value unchanged.
    The third always block triggers on the positive edge of the clock signal or the positive edge of the reset signal. It handles the hours value (Hours). If the reset signal is active, it sets the hours value to 0. If the hours, minutes, and seconds values are all at their maximum (23, 59, and 59 respectively), it wraps around and sets the hours value to 0. If the minutes and seconds values are both 59, it increments the hours value by 1. Otherwise, it keeps the hours value unchanged.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="CLK">Clock input</input>
        <input id="RST">Active high reset signal</input>
        <output id="Hours" width_description="[5:0]">6-bit output representing the current hours</output>
        <output id="Mins" width_description="[5:0]">6-bit output representing the current minutes</output>
        <output id="Secs" width_description="[5:0]">6-bit output representing the current seconds</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="MAX_SECONDS">59</parameter>
        <parameter id="MAX_MINUTES">59</parameter>
        <parameter id="MAX_HOURS">23</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg id="Secs_reg" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[5:0]">6-bit register to store the current seconds value.</reg>
        <reg id="Mins_reg" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[5:0]">6-bit register to store the current minutes value.</reg>
        <reg id="Hours_reg" type="sequential_logic" width_description="[5:0]">6-bit register to store the current hours value.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
    <always>
        <trigger>posedge CLK or posedge RST</trigger>
        <if_statement>
            <condition>RST</condition>
            <then>
                <assign>Secs = 6'b000000;</assign>
            </then>
            <else>
                <if_statement>
                    <condition>Secs == 6'b111011</condition>
                    <then>
                        <assign>Secs = 6'b000000;</assign>
                    </then>
                    <else>
                        <assign>Secs = Secs + 1;</assign>
                    </else>
                </if_statement>
            </else>
        </if_statement>
    </always>
    <always>
        <trigger>posedge CLK or posedge RST</trigger>
        <if_statement>
            <condition>RST</condition>
            <then>
                <assign>Mins = 6'b000000;</assign>
            </then>
            <else>
                <if_statement>
                    <condition>&Mins == 6'b111011 && Secs == 6'b111011</condition>
                    <then>
                        <assign>Mins = 6'b000000;</assign>
                    </then>
                    <else>
                        <if_statement>
                            <condition>Secs == 6'b111011</condition>
                            <then>
                                <assign>Mins = Mins + 1;</assign>
                            </then>
                            <else>
                                <assign>Mins = Mins;</assign>
                            </else>
                        </if_statement>
                    </else>
                </if_statement>
            </else>
        </if_statement>
    </always>
    <always>
        <trigger>posedge CLK or posedge RST</trigger>
        <if_statement>
            <condition>RST</condition>
            <then>
                <assign>Hours = 6'b000000;</assign>
            </then>
            <else>
                <if_statement>
                    <condition>&Hours == 6'b101101 && Mins == 6'b111011 && Secs == 6'b111011</condition>
                    <then>
                        <assign>Hours = 6'b000000;</assign>
                    </then>
                    <else>
                        <if_statement>
                            <condition>&Mins == 6'b111011 && Secs == 6'b111011</condition>
                            <then>
                                <assign>Hours = Hours + 1;</assign>
                            </then>
                            <else>
                                <assign>Hours = Hours;</assign>
                            </else>
                        </if_statement>
                    </else>
                </if_statement>
            </else>
        </if_statement>
    </always>
    </implementation>
</module>

module signal_generator
(
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    output reg [4:0] wave
);

    reg [1:0] state;
    always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if (!rst_n) begin
            state <= 2'b0;
            wave <= 5'd0;
        end else begin
            case (state)
                2'b0: begin
                    if (wave == 5'd31) begin
                        state <= 2'b1;
                    end else begin
                        wave <= wave + 1;
                    end
                end
                2'b1: begin
                    if (wave == 5'd0) begin
                        state <= 2'b0;
                    end else begin
                        wave <= wave - 1;
                    end
                end
            endcase
        end
    end
endmodule

<module id="signal_generator">
    <description>Implement a Triangle Wave signal generator module that generates a waveform by incrementing and decrementing a 5-bit signal named "wave". The waveform cycles between 0 and 31, which is incremented or decremented by 1.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <output id="wave" width_description="[4:0]">5-bit output waveform signal representing the generated waveform.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="state">State of the signal generator.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
        <sequential_logic>
            <reg id="state" width_description="[1:0]">State of the signal generator.</reg>
            <reg id="wave" width_description="[4:0]">5-bit output waveform signal representing the generated waveform.</reg>
            <always>
                <case>
                    <case_item id="state" value="0">
                        <item>
                            <assign id="wave" operation="<<" value="1"/> Increment wave by 1.
                        </item>
                    </case_item>
                    <case_item id="wave" value="31">
                        <item>
                            <assign id="state" value="1"/> Transition state to 1.
                        </item>
                    </case_item>
                    <case_item id="state" value="1">
                        <item>
                            <assign id="wave" operation="<<" value="1"/> Increment wave by 1.
                        </item>
                    </case_item>
                    <case_item id="wave" value="0">
                        <item>
                            <assign id="state" value="0"/> Transition state to 0.
                        </item>
                    </case_item>
                </case>
            </always>
        </sequential_logic>
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="accu">
    <description>Accumulate input data in 4 cycles and output the result.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock input for synchronization.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input data for addition.</input>
        <input id="valid_in">Input signal indicating readiness for new data.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Output signal indicating when 4 input data accumulation is reached.</output>
        <output id="data_out" width_description="[9:0]">10-bit output data representing the accumulated sum.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter name="IDLE">0</parameter>
        <parameter name="INPUT">1</parameter>
        <parameter name="ACCUMULATE">2</parameter>
        <parameter name="OUTPUT">3</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="state" width_description="[1:0]">State register for the accumulator.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="count" width_description="[1:0]">Counter register for input data.</reg>
        <reg type="sequential_logic" id="sum" width_description="[9:0]">Accumulated sum register.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        The module operates in a state machine fashion with four states: IDLE, INPUT, ACCUMULATE, and OUTPUT. 
        In the IDLE state, the state waits for valid_in to be high to transition to the INPUT state. 
        In the INPUT state, it accumulates input data_in values until the count reaches 3 (two cycles for four input data). 
        Once the count reaches 3, it transitions to the ACCUMULATE state to calculate the accumulated sum. 
        The accumulated sum is then output in the OUTPUT state, and the state transitions back to IDLE. 
        The valid_out signal is set to 1 during the OUTPUT state to indicate that the output is valid. 
    </implementation>
</module>

module JC_counter
(
    input clk,
    input rst_n
);
always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) begin
        Q <= 64'b0;
    end else begin
        if (Q[0] == 1'b0) begin
            Q <= {1'b1, Q[63:1]};
        end else begin
            Q <= {1'b0, Q[63:1]};
        end
    end
end
endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="JC_counter">
    <description>Implement a 64-bit Johnson counter (torsional ring counter).</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset.</input>
        <output id="Q" width_description="[63:0]">Current count value.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="N">64</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
    The counter has N bits, and the next state is either the current state shifted right by one with a 1 appended to the most significant bit if the least significant bit is 0, or the current state shifted right by one with a 0 appended to the most significant bit if the least significant bit is 1.
    </implementation>
</module>

module RAM
#(
    parameter WIDTH = 6,
    parameter DEPTH = 8
)
(
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    input write_en,
    input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] write_addr,
    input [WIDTH-1:0] write_data,
    input read_en,
    input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] read_addr,
    output reg [WIDTH-1:0] read_data
);
reg [WIDTH-1:0] RAM [DEPTH-1:0];
always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) begin
        for (integer i = 0; i < DEPTH; i = i + 1) begin
            RAM[i] <= 6'b000000;
        end
    end else if (write_en) begin
        RAM[write_addr] <= write_data;
    end
end
always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) begin
        read_data <= 6'b000000;
    end else if (read_en) begin
        read_data <= RAM[read_addr];
    end else begin
        read_data <= 6'b000000;
    end
end
endmodule
samples.xml_description:
Implement a dual-port RAM with a depth of 8 and a bit width of 6 bits, with all data initialized to 000000. It has two groups of ports, respectively for reading data and writing data, and read and write operations can be carried out at the same time. When the read_en signal is 1, the read_data of the corresponding position is read through the read_addr signal and output; When the write_en signal is 1, data is written to the corresponding position through the write_addr signal and write-data signal. Clock signal used for synchronous operation. Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive. Write enable signal to initiate a write operation. Address for the write operation. Data to be written to the RAM. Read enable signal to initiate a read operation. Address for the read operation. Output signal representing the data read from the RAM. 6 8 Read data register.

Define a 8-bit RAM array with a depth of 6 bits, initialized to 000000.

Define two always blocks, one for write operations and one for read operations.

On the positive edge of the clock signal or the negative edge of the reset signal, perform the following:

  1. Reset all memory locations in the RAM array to 0 if the reset signal is active.
  2. Write data to the RAM array at the specified write address if the write enable signal is active.

On the positive edge of the clock signal or the negative edge of the reset signal, perform the following:

  1. Reset the read data register to 0 if the reset signal is active.
  2. Read data from the RAM array at the specified read address if the read enable signal is active.

    <description>Implement a right shifter. The module performs an 8-bit right shift on a 1-bit input by first initializing the q register to 0. On each rising edge of the clock, the module shifts the contents of the q register to the right by one bit and inserts the new input bit d into the most significant position of the register.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock.</input>
        <input id="d">Input.</input>
        <output id="q" width_description="[7:0]">Output.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter name="register">q</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>Initialize the register to 0. Shift the contents of the register to the right by one bit on each rising edge of the clock. Insert the new input bit d into the most significant position of the register.</implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
<module id="synchronizer">
    <description>Implement a multi-bit MUX-based synchronizer.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk_a">Clock signal A.</input>
        <input id="clk_b">Clock signal B.</input>
        <input id="arstn">Active-low reset signal A.</input>
        <input id="brstn">Active-low reset signal B.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[3:0]">Input data signal.</input>
        <input id="data_en">Input enable signal.</input>
        <output id="dataout" width_description="[3:0]">Output data signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="data_reg">Register to store input data.</parameter>
        <parameter id="en_data_reg">Register to store input enable signal.</parameter>
        <parameter id="en_clap_one">Control register for selection of input data.</parameter>
        <parameter id="en_clap_two">Control register for selection of input data.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description>
